pattern: Segment tree with point updates: maintain a bit array of current peak flags and answer range-sum queries with O(log n) per operation.
hints: A peak at index i satisfies nums[i-1] < nums[i] > nums[i+1]. After a point update at index idx, only peak flags at idx-1, idx, idx+1 can change — recompute those three.
Store peak flags in a Fenwick tree (BIT) for O(log n) prefix-sum queries. For a type-1 query on [l, r], count peaks in indices [l+1, r-1] since endpoints can never be peaks.
For each point update, compute the change in peak flags at the three affected positions and apply delta updates to the BIT.

edge_inputs.description: query length-3 subarray — middle can be peak
update makes a non-peak into a peak
equal adjacent values: not a strict peak

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(3, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    q = rng.randint(1, size)
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        t = rng.randint(1, 2)
        if t == 1:
            l = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
            r = rng.randint(l, n - 1)
            queries.append([1, l, r])
        else:
            idx = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
            val = rng.randint(1, 100000)
            queries.append([2, idx, val])
    return [nums, queries]
